result.summary: The page is mostly visually coherent, but the bottom of the promo area appears broken: fixed/overlay elements cover the fine print, and the legal text is extremely hard to read. There are also a few minor layout/cropping issues around decorative imagery and the header.

issues.description: The “SCROLL DOWN” overlay sits directly on top of the legal/fine-print text near the bottom of the yellow promo section, obscuring content and making the area look cluttered.
issues.suggestion: Move the scroll prompt above or below the legal copy, reduce its size, or hide it once the user reaches the bottom portion of the hero content.

issues.description: The legal disclaimer text is extremely small and low-contrast against the yellow background, making it effectively unreadable on mobile.
issues.suggestion: Increase the font size, improve contrast, add more line spacing, and consider placing legal text in a dedicated expandable section or a clearer white/neutral container.

issues.description: The fixed bottom navigation visually competes with and nearly touches the legal text area, making the page feel cramped and potentially covering content on smaller screens.
issues.suggestion: Add bottom padding to the main content equal to the height of the fixed nav, and ensure the legal text fully clears the bottom navigation.

issues.description: Some decorative promotional elements appear awkwardly cropped at the edges, especially the baseball on the right and coins on the left, which can make the composition feel unintentionally clipped.
issues.suggestion: Adjust the positioning or scale of edge decorations so they are either intentionally cropped or fully visible with adequate margins.

issues.description: The top header contains several logos and labels packed tightly together, making the branding area feel crowded on mobile.
issues.suggestion: Reduce the number of logos shown at once, resize them, or add more spacing to improve visual clarity.
